EIA: Crude Oil Inventories in US See Rare Build
headlines.summary: Crude oil inventories in the United States saw a surprise increase of 3.0 million barrels during the week ending July 3, according to new data from the U.S. Energy Information Administration (EIA) released on Wednesday. headlines.title: Vitol Eyes Venezuela Expansion as Oil Industry Returns
headlines.summary: Commodity trading major Vitol is planning to establish a presence in Venezuela amid a surge in interest in the country’s oil wealth from the resources industry, Reuters has reported, citing unnamed sources. headlines.title: 63 Million Barrels Of Crude Stuck At Sea As U.S. Pulls Iran Sanction Waiver
headlines.summary: Iran was struggling to sell its crude to buyers in Asia outside China even before the U.S. rescinded overnight the sanctions waiver that allowed Tehran to sell its oil without penalties. headlines.title: Oil Prices Surge 6% Even as Tankers Push Through Hormuz
headlines.summary: Thin tanker traffic through the Strait of Hormuz continued early on Wednesday despite the fresh flare-up in the Middle East, but more vessels appear to be reconsidering moving toward the chokepoint after the U.S. and Iran traded new attacks overnight. At the time of writing, oil

headlines.title: Oil and LNG Tankers Make U-Turns After Fresh Hormuz Escalation
headlines.summary: The renewed hostilities and attacks on vessels in the Strait of Hormuz again put an abrupt end to the steady flow of oil and gas tankers moving in and out of the Persian Gulf. headlines.title: China Lifts Fuel Export Curbs as Refiners Ramp Up Overseas Shipments
headlines.summary: China has lifted restrictions on the exports of refined fuels, allowing state-owned refiners to resume shipments overseas this month, as well as one private refiner, Reuters reported today, citing unnamed sources. headlines.title: Oil Prices Jump After Iran Attacks Commercial Vessels
headlines.summary: Crude oil prices moved higher today, extending gains from earlier this week after the United States and Iran exchanged military strikes again, following Iran’s attacks on three vessels off the Omani coast. headlines.title: The Next Oil Price Spike Could Come Sooner Than Traders Think
headlines.summary: Oil prices have dropped back to pre-war levels since the United States and Iran agreed to negotiate a deal under a framework that included the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz. headlines.title: US Crude Oil, Product Inventories Fall Even As Hormuz Traffic Begins to Flow
headlines.summary: The American Petroleum Institute (API) estimated that crude oil inventories in the United States fell by 399,000 barrels in the week ending July 3.
